item.title: Regeneron
item.description:
• Summary: Regeneron announced a new regulatory indication for its flagship immunology drug and updates on genetics research. The company gained approval for a new use of Dupixent (dupilumab), an IL-4Rα inhibitor, likely in a chronic inflammatory condition. Regeneron also shared insights from its large-scale genetics research initiatives.
  • MoAs: Dupilumab: interleukin-4 receptor alpha (IL-4Rα) antagonist.
  • Sources: STAT News, STAT News

item.title: AbbVie
item.description:
• Summary: AbbVie navigated biosimilar competition for its top-selling drug and advanced its neurology pipeline. The company addressed the market impact of biosimilars for its anti-TNFα blockbuster Humira (adalimumab). Simultaneously, AbbVie provided updates on its neurology development programs, which may include therapies for conditions like migraine or Parkinson's disease.
  • MoAs: Adalimumab: tumor necrosis factor-alpha (TNFα) inhibitor.
  • Sources: STAT News, STAT News

item.title: Sanofi
item.description:
• Summary: Recent articles cover Sanofi's commercial and research activities. One focuses on the continued strong sales growth of its flagship immunology drug, Dupixent (dupilumab), an IL-4/IL-13 receptor antagonist, highlighting its market expansion across approved indications like atopic dermatitis and asthma. Another article details Sanofi's ongoing investments and strategic focus in its vaccine research division, aiming to advance new candidates against various infectious diseases.
  • MoAs: Dupilumab (Dupixent): IL-4/IL-13 receptor alpha antagonist.
  • Sources: STAT News, STAT News

item.title: Autoimmune Diseases
item.description:
• Summary: Autoimmune diseases are treated with biologics targeting immune pathways, such as adalimumab (anti-TNF) and secukinumab (anti-IL-17). New targets include JAK inhibitors like tofacitinib for conditions like rheumatoid arthritis.
  • MoAs: Adalimumab inhibits tumor necrosis factor; secukinumab blocks interleukin-17; tofacitinib interferes with JAK-STAT cytokine signaling.
  • Sources: Stat News, Stat News
